review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the NIST AC-3(9) control, focusing on controlled release of information through access enforcement. The functions map correctly to Kubernetes security features such as RBAC and Network Policies. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the function 'k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted' could be more descriptive to indicate the specific restriction being enforced.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted' to 'k8s_service_loadbalancer_exposure_restricted' for clarity on what is being restricted.
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | controlled release release information outside of the syste
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(9) focuses on controlled release of information outside the system, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that access is tightly controlled through RBAC and network policies. RBAC ensures that users and service accounts do not have excessive privileges, while network policies ensure that pods cannot communicate freely with external networks unless explicitly allowed. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services can prevent unintended exposure of services to the internet.
